headline: Bumiputera Economic Equity Stagnates at 17.2% After 55 Years
context: EPU's 2025 review showed Bumiputera corporate equity ownership at 17.2%, barely changed from 16.2% in 2010, despite over RM500 billion in cumulative policy support since the New Economic Policy began in 1971.

cards.big: 55 years and RM500 billion later — Bumiputera equity stuck at 17.2% versus the 30% NEP target
cards.sub: Policy has created a Bumiputera elite of 2,400 families holding 68% of allocated equity — not broad-based wealth

cards.big: Top 1% of Bumiputera shareholders hold RM84 billion while bottom 60% hold less than RM8 billion combined
cards.sub: Amanah Saham Bumiputera accounts averaging RM4,200 per holder provide minimal wealth-building impact

cards.big: Non-Bumiputera resentment at 78% in 2025 Merdeka Center poll — up from 58% in 2015 — risks social cohesion
cards.sub: Perceived unfairness of equity policy creates parallel economy where race trumps merit in business access

cards.big: TERAJU has disbursed RM14 billion in Bumiputera business development since 2011 but tracks no ROI metrics
cards.sub: Zero public reporting on how many TERAJU-funded businesses survive past 5 years or create sustainable jobs

cards.big: After 55 years, the honest question is whether this policy uplifts the many or enriches the connected few
Should Bumiputera equity policy shift from ownership targets to income and education outcomes instead?
